proposed_hypotheses.text: The 33 anonymized features fall into three structural groups (categorical/binary covariates, continuous labs, and a small number of balanced binary indicators that look like randomized treatment assignments), and pfs_months is a continuous outcome with no censoring indicator.

analyses.code: df=pd.read_parquet('dataset.parquet'); df.dtypes; df.describe(include='all'); df.nunique()
analyses.result_summary: 50000 rows x 35 columns. patient_id is a unique key. feature_006 is binary categorical (adenocarcinoma 72%, squamous 28%). feature_001 is 3-level categorical (former 55%, current 30%, never 15%). feature_014 is ordinal 0/1/2 (mean 0.80) consistent with ECOG. feature_015 is float 30-90 (mean 65) consistent with age. Continuous labs include feature_007 (128-152, sodium-like), feature_009 (7.3-11.8, hemoglobin-like), feature_025 (1.7-5.5, albumin-like), feature_033 (2.5-6.3, WBC-like). Binary candidate biomarkers/treatments include feature_011 (25%), feature_022 (13%), feature_016 (13%), feature_028 (5%), feature_002 (15%), feature_005 (3%), feature_023 (65%), feature_018 (35%), feature_021 (28%), feature_020 (30%), feature_027 (30%), feature_012 (50%), feature_031 (45%). pfs_months ranges 0-15.33 (mean 3.43, median 3.32) with no missing values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (age 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months (older patients have longer pfs_months).
feature_014 (ordinal 0/1/2, ECOG-like) is negatively associated with pfs_months (worse performance status, shorter pfs_months).
Current smokers (feature_001=='current') have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
Squamous histology (feature_006=='squamous') has shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
feature_023=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0.
feature_011=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_011=0.
feature_016=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_016=0.
feature_018=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_018=0 (candidate treatment).

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.769 between feature_015 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). Decile means rise monotonically from 0.74 months in the youngest decile to 6.55 in the oldest. Pearson r = +0.757.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_014')['pfs_months'].mean(); ANOVA across the three levels.
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_014: 0->4.33, 1->3.19, 2->2.15 (n=17557/24962/7481). ANOVA p<1e-300; difference between max and min levels = -2.18 months.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['pfs_months'].mean(); one-way ANOVA
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: never 3.67, former 3.61, current 2.99. ANOVA p=3.65e-187. Current smokers ~0.68 months shorter than never smokers.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']=='squamous','pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']=='adenocarcinoma','pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: adenocarcinoma 3.67, squamous 2.81. Difference = -0.86 months (Welch t-test p<1e-300).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_023']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_023']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_023=0 -> 4.36, =1 -> 2.92 (n=17635/32365). Difference = -1.44 months (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_011']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_011']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_011=0 -> 3.66, =1 -> 2.74 (n=37416/12584). Difference = -0.92 months (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_016']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_016']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_016=0 -> 3.33, =1 -> 4.11 (n=43632/6368). Difference = +0.78 months (p=7.4e-94).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_018']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_018']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_018=0 -> 3.32, =1 -> 3.64 (n=32456/17544). Difference = +0.32 months (p=5.6e-49).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all 33 features in a multivariable linear model, feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006 (squamous), feature_016, feature_017, feature_025, feature_018 and current smoking status remain independently associated with pfs_months in the directions seen in iteration 2; feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_022, feature_027 lose significance (likely non-treatment, non-prognostic indicators or randomized treatments with no main effect).

analyses.code: sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[ALL_FEATURES_INCLUDING_DUMMIES])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2 = 0.894. Top adjusted coefficients (|t| sorted): feature_015 +0.168 per year (t=512), feature_014 -1.10 per level (t=-230), feature_023 -1.42 (t=-208), feature_011 -0.91 (t=-122), hist_squamous -0.72 (t=-95), feature_016 +0.89 (t=+90), feature_017 -0.073 per unit (t=-87), feature_025 +0.46 per unit (t=+71), feature_018 +0.32 (t=+47), smk_current -0.55 (t=-44), feature_031 -0.20 (t=-31), feature_032 -4.5e-4 (t=-11), feature_005 -0.11 (t=-5.8), feature_002 -0.019 (t=-2.1). feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_022, feature_027 and most other continuous labs are not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (the most likely treatment indicator) is randomly assigned with prevalence ~35% within every key biomarker stratum (feature_016, feature_031, feature_028, feature_005), consistent with a balanced randomized assignment rather than channeled prescribing.

Other balanced binary indicators feature_012 (50%), feature_020 (30%), feature_027 (30%), feature_021 (28%) have no main effect on pfs_months in adjusted analysis and so are either non-treatment indicators or treatments with no average benefit.

analyses.code: df.groupby(biomarker)['feature_018'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: P(feature_018=1) = 0.351 in feature_016=0 vs 0.353 in feature_016=1; 0.354 vs 0.347 by feature_031; 0.351 vs 0.347 by feature_028; 0.351 vs 0.348 by feature_005. Treatment is balanced across all candidate effect-modifier strata.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient of each candidate treatment in the full multivariable model.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effects on pfs_months: feature_012 -0.006 (p=0.39), feature_020 +0.003 (p=0.74), feature_027 -0.002 (p=0.84), feature_021 -0.006 (p=0.41), feature_022 +0.001 (p=0.94), feature_002 -0.020 (p=0.033). None show clinically meaningful main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the candidate treatments (feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_027, feature_021, feature_022, feature_002, feature_028), feature_018 will show the strongest treatment-by-feature interaction signals; specifically, the feature_018 effect on pfs_months will be much larger in feature_016=1 patients and much smaller (or absent) in feature_031=1 patients.

analyses.code: For each (treatment, binary modifier) pair, fit OLS adjusted for base covariates with treatment*modifier interaction; rank by p-value of the interaction coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interactions in adjusted whole-cohort screen: feature_018 x feature_016 = +2.535 (p<1e-300); feature_018 x feature_031 = -0.585 (p<1e-300); feature_028 x feature_016 = -0.919 (p=6e-76); feature_018 x smk_current = +0.253 (p=4e-65); feature_018 x feature_028 = -0.343 (p=2e-27); feature_018 x feature_022 = -0.208 (p=8e-25); feature_018 x feature_005 = -0.328 (p=2e-16); feature_018 x hist_squamous = +0.108 (p=1.5e-12). All other interactions are an order of magnitude smaller.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratifying by feature_016, the adjusted treatment effect of feature_018 on pfs_months is essentially zero in feature_016=0 patients and substantially positive in feature_016=1 patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS of feature_018 within each feature_016 stratum.
analyses.result_summary: feature_016=0 (n=43632): feature_018 adjusted effect = +0.001 (SE 0.003, p=0.75) - effectively null. feature_016=1 (n=6368): feature_018 adjusted effect = +2.529 (SE 0.033, p<1e-300). The entire average treatment benefit is concentrated in the 12.7% of patients with feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1, the feature_018 treatment effect is further restricted to feature_031=0 patients; feature_031=1 fully suppresses the benefit even when feature_016=1.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS of feature_018 within feature_016 x feature_031 cells.
analyses.result_summary: feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 (n=3508): feature_018 adjusted effect = +4.586 (SE 0.024, p<1e-300). feature_016=1 & feature_031=1 (n=2860): feature_018 adjusted effect = +0.011 (SE 0.014, p=0.44). feature_016=0 cells (both feature_031 strata): adjusted effect ~0 (p>0.6). feature_031 acts as a complete suppressor of the feature_016-driven treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The whole-cohort feature_018 x smk_current interaction (+0.253, p=4e-65) is mediated entirely by feature_016 enrichment in current smokers; once feature_016 is conditioned on, smoking status no longer modifies the treatment effect.

Within the candidate responder subgroup feature_016=1 & feature_031=0, two further binary biomarkers (feature_028, feature_005) and possibly age (feature_015) modify the magnitude of the feature_018 treatment effect.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS of feature_018 within feature_016 x smk_current cells.
analyses.result_summary: feature_016=0 & smk_current=0 (n=31605): +0.001 (p=0.70). feature_016=0 & smk_current=1 (n=12027): +0.003 (p=0.72). feature_016=1 & smk_current=0 (n=3421): +2.516 (p<1e-300). feature_016=1 & smk_current=1 (n=2947): +2.549 (p<1e-300). The treatment effect is identical across smoking strata once feature_016 is conditioned on - smoking is not an independent modifier.

analyses.code: Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 (n=3508), fit OLS with feature_018 x EM interaction for each remaining binary and continuous feature.
analyses.result_summary: Significant within-subgroup interactions: feature_018 x feature_028 = -4.763 (p<1e-300); feature_018 x feature_005 = -4.799 (p<1e-300); feature_018 x feature_015 (age) = +0.028 per year (p=9e-34, modest scaling effect). Other binary features (feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_022, feature_023, feature_011, feature_002, hist_squamous, smk_current) and continuous features show no meaningful interaction inside this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The maximal feature_018 responder subgroup is feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; outside this subgroup, feature_018 has no detectable effect on pfs_months. Within it, feature_018 raises mean pfs_months by approximately 5 months.

analyses.code: Within the maximal subgroup, adjusted OLS for feature_018; in the complement, adjusted OLS for feature_018; crude means by feature_018.
analyses.result_summary: Maximal responder subgroup (n=3266, 6.5% of cohort): adjusted feature_018 effect = +4.925 months (SE 0.010, p<1e-300). Crude PFS: feature_018=0 -> 3.249 months (n=2112); feature_018=1 -> 8.234 months (n=1154); difference +4.985. Complement (n=46734): adjusted feature_018 effect = essentially 0 (mean PFS 3.32 vs 3.32). Adjusted effect with single suppressor on: feature_016=1 & feature_028=1 -> +0.025 (p=0.68); feature_016=1 & feature_005=1 -> -0.032 (p=0.65); feature_016=1 & feature_031=1 -> +0.011 (p=0.44). All three suppressors independently abolish the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A saturated linear model with three-way interactions feature_018 x feature_016 x {feature_031, feature_028, feature_005} should show: (i) feature_018 main coefficient ~0, (ii) feature_018 x feature_016 large positive (~+4.8), (iii) feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 large negative (~-4.6), (iv) feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_028 large negative (~-2.4), (v) feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_005 large negative (~-2.8). The model should explain >97% of variance.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ base_covariates + feature_018 + feature_016 + feature_031 + feature_028 + feature_005 + feature_016*feature_031 + feature_016*feature_028 + feature_016*feature_005 + feature_018*feature_016 + feature_018*feature_031 + feature_018*feature_016*feature_031 + feature_018*feature_016*feature_028 + feature_018*feature_016*feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Saturated model adjusted R^2 = 0.9752. Key coefficients: feature_018 main = +0.0018 (p=0.70); feature_018 x feature_016 = +4.768 (p<1e-300); feature_018 x feature_031 = -0.0018 (p=0.80); feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 = -4.586 (p<1e-300); feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_028 = -2.402 (p<1e-300); feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_005 = -2.773 (p<1e-300). All three-way suppressor interactions are highly significant; the treatment effect is fully captured by the feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0 cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the maximal feature_018 responder subgroup, none of the other balanced binary indicators (feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027, feature_002) shows a treatment-like effect on pfs_months, confirming that feature_018 is the only treatment with detectable benefit in this dataset.

analyses.code: Within max responder subgroup, adjusted OLS coefficient for each candidate treatment.
analyses.result_summary: Within the maximal responder subgroup (n=3266): feature_012 = -0.004 (p=0.63); feature_020 = +0.003 (p=0.77); feature_021 = -0.033 (p=9e-4) but tiny in magnitude; feature_027 = +0.005 (p=0.62); feature_002 = -0.001 (p=0.91). No other indicator approaches the +4.9 month feature_018 benefit. feature_018 is the only treatment with a clinically meaningful effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the maximal responder subgroup, age (feature_015) modestly increases the magnitude of the feature_018 treatment effect, but the absolute change across the age range is small (about +0.3 months from young to old tertile).

analyses.code: Within max subgroup, adjusted feature_018 effect by age tertile.
analyses.result_summary: Within max responder subgroup, by age tertile: young (32-61, n=1094) +4.764; mid (61-70, n=1086) +4.994; old (70-90, n=1086) +5.039. The treatment effect is large and clinically significant in every age tertile; age is a minor positive modifier but not a defining inclusion criterion.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the feature_018 x feature_016 x {feature_031, feature_028, feature_005} structure, no continuous lab feature (feature_017, feature_025, feature_032, feature_010, feature_024, feature_007, feature_009, feature_026, feature_013, feature_004, feature_008, feature_030, feature_033, feature_029, feature_003, feature_019) is a meaningful additional modifier of the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: For each continuous EM, fit OLS with feature_018 x EM interaction in the full cohort (adjusted).
analyses.result_summary: Whole-cohort interactions of feature_018 with continuous features (adjusted, p<0.01): only feature_015 (age) +0.0035 per year (p=3e-7). All other continuous features have interaction p>0.05 or near-zero coefficients. The age signal reflects the modest within-subgroup age modulation already documented in iteration 12.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: Treatment feature_018 increases mean pfs_months by approximately +4.93 months (adjusted; +4.99 crude) in the subgroup defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0. Outside this subgroup the feature_018 effect is null. feature_016=1 is the predictive eligibility criterion; feature_031=1, feature_028=1, and feature_005=1 each independently abolish the benefit and so must be exclusion criteria.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS within max subgroup with all base covariates and other treatment indicators included.
analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup (n=3266): adjusted feature_018 coefficient = +4.925 months (SE 0.0095, p<1e-300). Crude means: untreated 3.249 vs treated 8.234 months (delta +4.985, n=2112 vs 1154). All three exclusion biomarkers (feature_031, feature_028, feature_005) independently nullify the benefit when present, even with feature_016=1 (effects within those single-suppressor cells: +0.011, +0.025, -0.032 respectively, all p>0.4). This is the final, fully specified treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis.
